title: Ctenitopsis matthewii
intro: Ctenitopsis matthewii, also known as Matthew's fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. Research on Matthew's fern has shown that it possesses potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitopsis matthewii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Matthew's fern is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ctenitopsis matthewii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Ctenitopsis is part of the fern family and is found in Madagascar. Ctenitopsis matthewii, specifically, is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within this family. The classification of Ctenitopsis matthewii is further divided into kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. Within the kingdom Plantae, Ctenitopsis matthewii falls under the phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Ctenitopsis, and species C. matthewii.
